manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: petroleum, pooch, morale, guile, puck, enzyme, adultery, cavalry, inventor.
Your opponent's team words are: chimp, opponent, drifter, census, vial, courtroom, physicist, condom.
Distractor words are: brat, motorcade, assailant, slipper, ultimatum, hypocrite, broker.
The kill words are: checkup.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue that relates to some of our team's words, avoid opponent/kill/distractor. Provide clue and list of targets.

We have team words: petroleum, pooch, morale (maybe morale), guile, puck, enzyme, adultion, cavalry, inventor.

We want a clue that can cover multiple. Let's think:

Possible categories: animals: pooch (dog). cavalry (horses). petroleum (oil). enzyme (biology). puck (hockey). inventor (maybe Edison). morale (spirit). guile (cunning). adultery (infidelity). We want a clue with multiple connections.

Maybe "spirit" could relate to morale, petroleum (spirit is another word for alcohol derived from petroleum? Actually "spirit" can mean morale, also "spirit" as in ghost, not linking. Petroleum spirit = "mineral spirit" is a solvent. Could also relate to "guile" (spirit? Not really). "Inventor" maybe "Edison". "Cavalry" "horse". "Pooch" dog. "Puck" is also a mischievous sprite in Shakespeare (a spirit). Could clue "sprite"? Sprite: a spirit (mythical), also a soda (maybe petroleum? not). Sprite is also a fairy, not directly. Not good.

Maybe "oil" connects petroleum, and also "oil" can be used for lubrication of a gun (cavalry? not). Not.

We can use "Chemistry": enzyme, petroleum, maybe guile? Not.

"Seasoned": not.

"Game": puck (hockey), morale (team spirit), cavalry (maybe not). Not.

"Adventure": inventor, cavalry, guile, maybe.

We need to avoid clue containing parts of any words: cannot contain parts of any of the list words. So cannot include "petro", "oil", "pooch", etc. The clue must be a single word.

We might aim for 2-3 target words. Something like "hockey" would link puck, morale (team morale), cavalry? Not. Might be safe.

But need to ensure clue not contain any part of opponent/distractor words. The clue "hockey" is okay (no overlapping substring). Targets: puck, morale? Huh morale via team spirit? Might be ambiguous.

Better choose "dogged". dogged relates to pooch (dog) and morale (doggedness). But "dog" is part of pooch? Not same word but "dog" is a part of pooch? The rule: cannot contain parts of them. Might be considered substring? "dog" is not a substring of "pooch". So okay. But "dogged" contains "dog" which is related to pooch. Might be fine.

But we need to list target words; we can choose just pooch and morale perhaps.

Clue "faithful": relates to morale (faith), and pooch (dog is faithful). Could be.

Better to aim for 3: "horse". That ties to cavalry (horses), morale? Not. Petroleum? Not. Could also tie to "pouch"? no.

Maybe "engine". Connects petroleum (fuel), enzyme? not.

What about "invent": could connect inventor, guile (cunning), morale? Not.

Consider "spectrum". no.

We must avoid kill word "checkup". Our clue should not be "check".

Let's think of a clue that ties to "inventor", "guile", "cavalry". Maybe "Tactical". Tactical relates to cavalry, morale, guile (cunning). Could be.

But we need to be confident partner can guess.

Maybe simplest: give clue "Dog" relates to pooch only. But only one target; maybe need more? It's allowed any number. But we want efficiency.

We could give clue "Fuel" relates to petroleum and enzyme (energy). Enzyme catalyzes reactions releasing energy; not strong.

Better clue "Alcohol". Connects petroleum (mineral spirits), morale (spirits), guile (spirits? not). Might be fine.

But we must ensure not a part of any opponent or distractor words. "Alcohol" not overlapping.

Probably safer to give clue "Spirit". Connects morale (spirit), guile (spirit? maybe not), petroleum (spirit is a solvent). Also "puck"? There's a "spirit" in Shakespeare's A Midsummer Night's Dream (Puck is a spirit). That's clever! It relates to puck (the fairy spirit), morale (team spirit), petroleum (mineral spirit). So we could target: morale, puck, petroleum. Good.

Check: Do any opponent/distractor words contain "spirit"? No. So okay.

Thus answer:

CLUE: Spirit
TARGETS: morale, puck, petroleum

Make sure format exactly
